title: The Camp
summary: The home base, its routines, and why everything keeps circling back there.
intro: The camp is where the world exhales. Part refuge, part workshop, part social space, it should feel like more than a menu with firelight.
facts: A memorable camp should feel changed by what happened outside it.
The hub works best when narrative rest and practical upgrades sit close together.
Players come back for safety, but they stay for the people and the small changes.

sections.heading: Purpose
paragraphs: The camp is the reset point between expeditions, but it should also feel like the place where consequences finally settle in.
It gives the player room to plan, recover, and notice what changed after the last trip out.
items: Safe space for dialogue and downtime scenes.
Central location for upgrades and progression tracking.
A natural place to quietly restate what matters before the next outing.

sections.heading: Stations
paragraphs: Breaking the camp into stations keeps the place readable without flattening it. Each corner of the camp should hint at a habit, a person, or a routine.
items: Workshop for repair, tuning, and the practical side of staying alive.
Planning table for map review, route choices, and mission selection.
Firepit or commons area for quieter conversations and morale beats.

sections.heading: Growth Over Time
paragraphs: A camp becomes real when it visibly changes. New tools, repaired structures, and fresh ambient dialogue make progress feel lived in rather than logged.
items: Let story milestones leave visible marks on the space.
Unlock companion interactions through trust or quest completion.
Make new camp tools feed back into field exploration.
